title: Budget Tags — share visibility on a project
what: Tag teammates onto any project budget so it's clear who's working it. Each tagged person shows as a colored first/last-initial icon next to the budget's Status. Setting a Budget Owner automatically tags them, and tags drive the My Projects filter on the pipeline page.
where: ProFi → open any project budget → the header row next to Status (labeled "Tagged").
how: Open the project's budget in ProFi.
In the header row (Budget Dated / Owner / Status), find Tagged and click + Tag.
Pick a name — the list matches the Budget Owner dropdown. Their initials icon appears instantly.
Setting the Budget Owner tags that person automatically — no extra step.
Click an initials icon to remove someone (it asks to confirm).

title: Show My Projects filter on the Finance pipeline
what: One click filters the Project Finance list down to only the budgets you own (Budget Owner) or are tagged on — no more scrolling everyone else's projects.
where: ProFi → Project Finance page → top right, next to + New Project.
how: Open Project Finance from the hub.
Click Show My Projects (top right) — the button turns blue "✓ My Projects" and the list filters to yours.
It works together with the RFP / Live / Archive folders.
Click it again to see everything.

title: Hub: Project View / Operations View toggle
what: The hub now has two modes. Project View lists every project as a tile, organized by project code, with search. Operations View shows the three apps (ProFi, FreePro, AvocadoPost). Team Management stays as a full-width bar below both views. The hub remembers which view you used last.
where: The hub (home page) — the toggle sits centered under "Where to today?".
how: Sign in — the hub opens in the view you used last.
Click 🗂 Project View to browse all projects by code (use the search box for code, title, or client).
Click ⚙ Operations View to jump into ProFi, FreePro, or AvocadoPost directly.
Team Management is always available in the elongated blue tile below.

title: Project Workspace — Finance, Pre-Production & Post in one view
what: Opening a project from Project View lands in a single workspace where you flip between the full ProFi budget, the FreePro production page, and the AvocadoPost project page. These are the real pages, not copies — any change made here (budget lines, schedules, edits) is instantly the same everywhere.
where: Hub → Project View → click any project tile.
how: From the hub's Project View, click a project tile.
Use the Project Finance | Pre-Production | Post-Production toggle at the top right to flip sections.
If a budget has multiple productions, a shoot-code dropdown appears on the Pre-Production tab.
The Post-Production tab creates the project's Avo page automatically the first time you open it.
‹ All Projects returns to the project list.

title: Budget status paths (RFP → Live/Dead, Live → Reconcile/Closed)
what: The budget status dropdown now guides the workflow instead of listing every status. From RFP you can go Live or Dead; from Live you can go Reconcile or Closed; Reconcile moves to Closed. Archived budgets can be reopened (Closed → Live, Dead → RFP).
where: ProFi → any project budget → the Status pill in the header row.
how: Open a budget and click the Status pill.
Only the valid next steps for the current status are offered (with a hint under each).
Dead and Closed both send the budget to the Archive folder; closing also archives its FreePro shoot tiles.

title: Archive organized by Closed / Dead and by year
what: The Archive folder separates finished work from dead RFPs, and closed budgets are filed by year so the archive stays browsable as it grows.
where: ProFi → Project Finance → the Archive folder chip.
how: Open the Archive folder on the Project Finance page.
Use the Closed | Dead toggle — Closed is the default.
In Closed, pick a year from the scrollable chips (defaults to the current year; the year comes from the budget's close month).
Dead shows all not-approved budgets in one list.

title: Hub dashboard — Day in Review & Team Today
what: The lower half of the hub is now a live dashboard. The left panel is your personal Day in Review: shoots you're on today and anything due today on edits where you're the editor, PM, or task assignee. The right panel shows where every Unbridled team member is right now with a status bubble — red for Out of Office/PTO, yellow for traveling/on a shoot (with the shoot's city as their location), green for in office (St. Louis, or Denver for our Denver folks).
where: The hub (home page), below the tiles — visible in both Project View and Operations View.
how: Sign in — Day in Review builds itself from your assignments for today; click any row to jump to that shoot or edit.
You'll see items when you're on a shoot, when an edit or timeline milestone you own is due, when an edit window starts, or when you're on PTO.
Team Today lists the team with status bubbles: 🔴 PTO/OOO, 🟡 traveling/on a shoot (location = shoot city), 🟢 in office.
The Team Management button sits at the top right of the Team Today panel.

title: Reports section (Project Finance Overview moved here)
what: A new Reports area on the hub collects cross-project rollups. Project Finance Overview has moved out of ProFi into Reports, alongside the Weekly Finance Report.
where: Hub → the 📈 Reports button at the top, next to Crew Calendar.
how: Click 📈 Reports on the hub.
Pick a report card — Project Finance Overview (budgets, direct costs, gross profit per project) or Weekly Finance Report.
The overview is no longer on the ProFi page — this is its new home.

title: Vendor Invoices on the VCC (with AI reading)
what: Each project's VCC now has a Vendor Invoices library: upload the actual invoice files and they live with the project. When an invoice PDF or photo is uploaded, AI reads it and fills in the vendor name and invoice total automatically. The redundant Sync FreePro Costs button was removed (costs auto-sync).
where: ProFi → project budget → VCC tab → the 🧾 Vendor Invoices button on the right of the toolbar.
how: Open a project's VCC and click 🧾 Vendor Invoices.
Click + Upload Invoice and pick the file (PDF, photo, or spreadsheet — 20MB max).
For PDFs and photos, the vendor name and total appear on the row automatically after upload.
Click a filename (or ⬇) to download; ✕ deletes with a confirm.

title: Printer-friendly PDFs (shot lists & call sheets)
what: Exporting a shot list or call sheet now produces a clean, high-contrast report instead of a screen print: white pages, black text, no browser URL/title stamped at the top and bottom, interactive buttons removed, and rows kept together across page breaks.
where: Any shared view (shot list, call sheet) → the PDF button in the top bar, or your browser's Print.
how: Open the shot list (or call sheet) view and click PDF.
In the print dialog choose Save as PDF — margins and styling are handled for you.
The printed document is titled with the project code and name.
Scene color-coding (INT blue / EXT green) is kept for navigation; all text prints black.

title: Invoice Search report
what: A cross-project invoice finder in Reports. Search every uploaded vendor invoice by vendor name, project code, or exact invoice total, and click any result to preview the actual file in a pop-out viewer (PDFs and photos render inline).
where: Hub → 📈 Reports → Invoice Search.
how: Open Reports from the hub and click Invoice Search.
Type in any of the three filters — Vendor (matches the AI-read vendor name or the filename), Project Code, or Invoice Total (exact amount). Results update as you type.
Click a row to pop out a quick view of the invoice file; use ⬇ Download to save it.
Invoices get here automatically — anything uploaded on a project's VCC → Vendor Invoices is searchable.

title: Budget Overview (renamed, counts crew correctly)
what: The client-facing summary on each budget is now called Budget Overview (it stays "Estimate Overview" on estimates). Cost Inclusions now count crew members — one line item equals one person, shown as "2x Director" style when a role repeats — instead of counting billable days. On budgets in Estimate mode, the overview pulls live from the estimate you're viewing, including unsaved edits.
where: ProFi → project budget → the Budget Overview button (top of the budget); in Estimate mode, the Estimate Overview button.
how: Open a budget and click Budget Overview.
Each section lists its estimate total and the crew/services included — headcount, not days.
📋 Copy for Email pastes the formatted table straight into Outlook or Gmail.
In Estimate mode, the same button reads Estimate Overview and reflects the estimate exactly as it's currently shown.

title: Project Hub & Client Hub on Project View
what: Project View is now split into two sections, each with its own search bar. Project Hub lists every project as a tile. Client Hub gives every client a mini-hub: all of their projects in one place plus a Client Resources library for logos, brand guidelines, and other shared files. Each client hub also has a Client View toggle — the future client portal (coming soon) — which marks the page with an orange border so it's obvious you're looking at what the client will see.
where: Hub → Project View toggle (or the standalone Project View page). Client tiles sit below the project tiles.
how: Switch the hub to Project View — Project Hub is at the top with its search bar beside it.
Scroll to Client Hub and use its own search bar to find a client, then click their tile.

Inside the mini-hub, click any project tile to open its full workspace, or use Client Resources to upload the client's logo and brand guidelines (logos show as thumbnails; everything is downloadable).
Flip the Team View | Client View toggle to preview the client portal — the orange top border means you're in client-access view.

title: Client roster with duplicate detection
what: The Client field on budgets is now a searchable dropdown backed by a company-wide client roster, so every project uses the exact same client spelling (which keeps Client Hubs from splitting). New names can be added on the spot — and if a new name looks too much like an existing client (typo, abbreviation, alternate spelling), AI flags it as a possible duplicate before it's added, with a one-click override if it really is a different client.
where: ProFi → + New Project, or any budget → ✎ Edit → the Client field.
how: Start typing in the Client field — matching roster clients appear; click one to select it.
If the client isn't listed, click + Add “…” as a new client.
If the name resembles an existing client, a ⚠ Possible duplicate panel appears: click Use “…” to pick the existing client, or It's a different client — add anyway to override.
The roster started pre-loaded with every client name already on your projects.

title: Client Hub password
what: Each client hub now has its own password, set from a button right of the Team View / Client View toggle. This is the password clients will use to open their portal once the Client View ships — set it now and it's ready when the portal is.
where: Project View → any client's hub → the 🔒 button next to the view toggle.
how: Open a client's hub and look right of the Team View | Client View toggle.
Click 🔒 Set Hub Password and type the password — the button turns green (🔒 Password Set).
Click it again to change the password, or leave the prompt empty to remove it.

title: Drive field on the edit form
what: Each AvocadoPost edit now records which drive its media lives on, right next to Category on the edit form. Changes are tracked in the edit's activity feed like every other field.
where: AvocadoPost → open any edit → the Drive field beside Category.
how: Open an edit from the pipeline.
Type the drive name or number in Drive (next to Category) — it saves automatically when you click away.

title: Customizable grids on Avo project pages
what: Every tab on an Avo project page (Video Tracker, To-Do List, Music Options, Lower Thirds) is now a customizable grid: add your own text columns, merge cells into blocks, drag rows and columns into any order, and add rows from every tab — including the Video Tracker, where a new row creates a real pipeline edit. Layouts are saved per project.
where: AvocadoPost → Project Lookup → open a project → any tab. The toolbar sits under each grid.
how: + Add Row — on the Video Tracker it asks for a title and creates a pipeline edit under this project's code.
+ Add Column — name it and a text column appears; hover its header for ✎ rename / ✕ remove.
Merge Cells — click the button, click the first and last cell of the block (any rectangle), then ✓ Apply Merge. Click a merged cell in merge mode to Unmerge.
Drag the ⠿ grip to reorder rows; drag a column header onto another to reorder columns.
Everything (columns, merges, order) is remembered per project page.

title: Custom tables on Avo project pages
what: Build your own tables on any Avo project page. + Add Custom Table creates a new tab next to the built-in four, starting with one column — from there it has the full grid toolkit: add rows and columns, merge cells, drag to reorder, and per-column filters. Each project keeps its own set of tables.
where: AvocadoPost → open a project page → + Add Custom Table at the end of the tab row.
how: Click + Add Custom Table and name it (e.g. "Graphics Requests") — it opens as a new tab.
Use + Add Row / + Add Column to build it out; every cell is editable text.
Merge, drag-reorder, and filter work exactly like the built-in tabs.
✎ Rename Table and ✕ Delete Table sit under the grid.

title: Column filters on every Avo grid
what: Every column header on every Avo grid — built-in tabs and custom tables — has a small ⧩ filter icon. Click it for a dropdown that narrows rows to matches as you type. Filter several columns at once; active filters tint their icon green and the toolbar shows the match count with a one-click reset.
where: Any Avo project page tab → the ⧩ icon beside each column name.
how: Click the ⧩ icon on a column and type in the Contains… box — rows filter live.
Enter or Done closes the dropdown; Clear resets that column.
Stack filters on multiple columns; the toolbar shows "N of M rows" and ✕ Clear Filters resets everything.
Row dragging and merging pause while a filter is hiding rows, and come right back when cleared.

title: Project Overview — cover page with call notes & to-dos
what: Every project in Project View now opens on an Overview tab: a cover page showing the budget status, each shoot with dates and status, post-production at a glance (every edit with its pipeline status), and a Client Call Notes section for dated takeaways from client calls. On the right is a project To-Do list for one-off tasks — checkbox, task, a tag for any Unbridled crew member, and a complete-by date, with a click-to-expand description on each task.
where: Hub → Project View → any project (Overview is the first tab and the default).
how: Open a project — the Overview loads first. Click any shoot or edit row to jump to its full page.
+ Add Note under Client Call Notes creates a dated entry; the date and text edit inline.
+ Add Task on the To-Do column adds a task: type it, pick a crew member in the Tag dropdown, set the complete-by date (red when overdue), and click ▸ to add a description.
Check the box when it's done — completed tasks strike through and drop to the bottom.

title: My Tasks on the hub dashboard
what: Tasks tagged to you on any Project Overview follow you home: the Day in Review panel on the hub now has a My Tasks section listing your open tasks with a checkbox, the task and its project, and the due date (red when overdue). Checking the box completes the task everywhere.
where: The hub (home page) → Day in Review panel, below today's items.
how: Sign in — any open task tagged to you appears under My Tasks.
Due dates show on the right; overdue dates are red.
Click the checkbox to mark it complete — it clears from your list and checks off on the project's Overview too.

title: Edit / override location addresses
what: Location addresses on a FreePro project are no longer locked to what the map search fills in. Every location row has an ✎ edit button that reopens the location form with the current details, so a wrong or outdated address can be corrected manually — the maps link and call sheets use your override.
where: FreePro → any project → Overview tab → Locations → the ✎ on a location row.
how: Click ✎ on the location row.
Fix the Address (or Name, Type, Emoji) — type over it manually, or re-run the place search.
Click Save Changes — the row, maps link, and anywhere the location appears now use the corrected address.

title: Reference photos on the Shot List
what: Every shot can now carry reference photos — framing references, location stills, storyboard frames. Upload from the shot's detail form, and a Reference Photos section at the bottom of the Shot List shows every photo in shot order, labeled with the shot number and title.
where: FreePro → project → Logistics → Shot List. Upload from a shot's expanded detail (or its pop-out on mobile); browse the Reference Photos section at the bottom of the page.
how: Open a shot's detail and click + Reference Photo to upload an image (15MB max).
Scroll to the bottom of the Shot List — photos appear as tiles in sequential shot order with "Shot 1A" and the shot's description.
Click a tile to view it full size; ✕ deletes it.

title: Additional Docs — reference documents on every shoot
what: A new Additional Docs tab under FreePro's Logistics dropdown stores reference documents for the shoot — lighting grids, show flows, floor plans, run-of-show decks. When documents exist, the producer and crew call sheet views grow a matching Additional Docs tab with tap-to-view links; with nothing uploaded, the tab stays out of the crew's nav entirely.
where: FreePro → project → Logistics → Additional Docs. Crew/producers see them on their shared call sheet views.
how: Open Logistics → Additional Docs and click + Upload Doc (any file type, 25MB max).
Click a row to view or download; ✕ deletes it.
On producer and crew share links, an Additional Docs tab appears automatically once at least one document exists.

title: Testing — Feedback & Features button
what: A big red button at the very top of the hub opens the team's one running checklist for testing feedback and feature requests. Anyone signed in — crew included — can add items, check them off when handled, and delete them. Each entry shows who added it and when, and the header counts what's still open. This is the only place the list lives.
where: The hub (home page) — the red button above everything else.
how: Click ! Testing - Feedback and Features ! at the top of the hub.
Type your feedback or feature request and press Add (or Enter).
Check an item off when it's been handled — it strikes through and drops below the open items.
✕ deletes an item (with a confirm).

title: Creative Docs on the Project Overview
what: The Overview's right column has a Creative Docs tile for the project's creative brief and VPP. Uploads show live preview thumbnails (images render directly, PDFs show their first page) and open in a pop-out viewer with a download button.
where: Hub → Project View → project → Overview tab → Creative Docs tile (above To-Do).
how: Click + Creative Brief or + VPP and pick the file (25MB max).
Each doc shows as a labeled preview card — click it to open the full pop-out viewer.
⬇ Download saves the original; the small ✕ on a card deletes it.

title: Overview budget tile — status, totals, and client identity
what: The Overview's budget tile is now a slim strip: Budget Status on the left, the running totals on the right — Budget (the live ProFi budget total) and Est Fee (the billable profit from the VCC: budget total minus vendor costs). The client's logo (or name) now sits above the project code at the top of the page.
where: Hub → Project View → project → Overview tab.
how: Budget $x uses the same math as the ProFi pipeline, live.
Est Fee $y matches the VCC Profit Summary — it moves as vendor costs land.
The client logo above the code comes from the budget's stored client logo; projects without one show the client name.

title: Task polish — initials chips, notes, and due badges
what: Project tasks got a round of polish. Assignees show as colored first/last-initial chips beside the task name (click to reassign or unassign). On the hub, My Tasks dates read “Due 7/9”, anything due today shows ❗ Due Today with an orange (!) Task Due Today badge on the section header, and clicking a task expands its description/notes — click the note to jump to the project's Overview.
where: Project Overview → To-Do column, and the hub's Day in Review → My Tasks.
how: On a task, click the initials chip (or dashed +) to pick a crew member from the roster.
On the hub, click a task's name to expand its description; click the panel to open the project.
Due dates show as “Due x/x”; overdue turns red and due-today shows ❗ Due Today.

title: Day in Review — today's date, Coming Tomorrow, and in-progress work
what: The Day in Review panel now shows today's date under its header, mirrors everything from the Crew Calendar that spans today (green “Edit in progress” and “Working: …” rows alongside shoots, due dates, and PTO), and previews the next day in a Coming Tomorrow section above My Tasks.
where: The hub (home page) → Day in Review panel.
how: Today's items list first — shoots, due milestones, edits in progress, PTO.
Coming Tomorrow (with tomorrow's date) previews the same signals for the next day, slightly dimmed.
Every row clicks through to its shoot or edit.

title: Team Today — two columns and STL/DEN Only handling
what: The Team Today roster now lists in two columns (one on phones), halving the tile's height. STL/DEN Only requests no longer mark someone as out — they stay green in-office with an “STL/DEN only” note, since it's a no-travel scheduling notation, not time off.
where: The hub (home page) → Team Today panel.
how: 🔴 PTO/OOO · 🟡 traveling/on a shoot · 🟢 in office.
Someone on an STL/DEN Only request shows green with “STL/DEN only” beside their office — bookable locally, not for travel.

title: Travel/Local flag on the crew roster
what: Every crew member can be flagged Travel (default) or Local. The toggle lives on the crew edit form, and the assigned-crew grid on each project shows a TRAVEL (blue) or LOCAL (gray) pill per person — one flag on the roster record, consistent across every project.
where: FreePro → project → Crew tab (form + grid), and the Roster Lookup in Team Management.
how: Open a crew member's edit form and flip the Travel / Local toggle next to Home Airport.
The Travel column on the crew grid (between Dietary and Start Date) shows each person's flag.
